items.briefing:
AI is making phishing scams more convincing and harder to spot, with criminals now running large-scale, professional-looking phishing operations targeting organisations worldwide. Separately, a Chinese cybercrime group known as TA4922 is expanding its attacks globally, which may include Australian targets. Australian organisations should be aware that AI-generated fraud attempts — including fake emails, voice calls, and identity scams — are increasing in sophistication.

Australia also leads the world in self-hosted AI adoption, meaning local organisations may face unique security risks from managing AI tools internally. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected email or message asking you to click a link or provide information, verify the request by contacting the sender directly through a known phone number — not the contact details in the message.

featured.title: Australia leads in self-hosted AI use, JFrog finds
featured.quote: Australia leads in self-hosted AI adoption but lacks critical security controls like secrets scanning.
